sources.title: The 2025 AI Index Report
sources.claim: Inference cost for GPT-3.5-level AI fell 280x — from $20/M tokens (Nov 2022) to $0.07/M tokens (Oct 2024, Gemini-1.5-Flash-8B)

sources.title: AI costs drop 280-fold: Stanford 2025 AI Report
sources.claim: Confirms Stanford AI Index 2025 finding: $20 → $0.07 per million tokens in 18 months

sources.title: Small Language Models: Gartner's 3x Edge AI Prediction
sources.claim: Gartner predicts organizations will use SLMs at least 3x more than general-purpose LLMs by 2027; 143.1M AI PC shipments projected for 2026

sources.title: Introducing Phi-4: Microsoft's Newest Small Language Model Specializing in Complex Reasoning
sources.claim: Phi-4 (14B) released December 12, 2024; outperforms larger models on STEM/reasoning benchmarks; available on Azure AI Foundry

sources.title: Introducing Gemma 3: The Developer Guide
sources.claim: Gemma 3 released March 12, 2025; available in 1B, 4B, 12B, 27B sizes; 140-language support; 4B model runs on 4.2GB RAM

sources.title: Introducing Apple's On-Device and Server Foundation Models
sources.claim: Apple's ~3B-parameter on-device Foundation Model powers Apple Intelligence; opened to third-party developers at WWDC 2025

sources.title: Apple Foundation Models Framework Unlocks New Intelligent App Experiences
sources.claim: Foundation Models framework available September 2025; enables any iOS/macOS app to call on-device SLM with no cloud dependency

sources.title: EU AI Act 2025 Review and 2026 Outlook
sources.claim: EU AI Act high-risk system requirements fully enforceable from August 2, 2026; GPAI providers must disclose energy consumption

sources.title: Energy Efficiency Requirements Under the EU AI Act
sources.claim: GPAI model providers required to disclose energy consumption; EU planning standardization deliverables for AI energy reporting in 2026

sources.title: Enterprise Guide to Small Language Models and Edge AI 2026
sources.claim: For 80% of production use cases a local SLM costs 95% less than cloud-based alternatives; 73% of organizations moving AI inference to edge

sources.title: The Power of Small: Edge AI Predictions for 2026
sources.claim: Edge AI and SLMs identified as key enterprise technology shifts in 2026; on-device inference avoids cloud latency and cost

sources.title: Small Language Models and Edge AI: The 2026 Shift to Local Intelligence
sources.claim: SLM edge deployment market projected to grow from $3.42B in 2025 to $12.85B by 2030 at 30.27% CAGR

sources.title: AI Inference's 280x Slide: 18-Month Cost Optimization Explained
sources.claim: Detailed breakdown of how SLM architecture improvements drove the 280x inference cost reduction from Nov 2022 to Oct 2024

sources.title: Key Edge AI Trends Transforming Enterprise Tech in 2026
sources.claim: 73% of organizations actively moving AI inferencing to edge environments; privacy and latency as primary drivers

sources.title: Gemma Releases Documentation
sources.claim: Gemma 1 (Feb 2024), Gemma 2 (Jun 2024), Gemma 3 (Mar 2025), Gemma 4 (Apr 2026) — official release history

sources.title: Best Small Language Models 2026: 12 SLMs Ranked for 8GB RAM
sources.claim: Phi-4 (14B) tops reasoning benchmarks; Gemma 3 4B runs on 4.2GB RAM; Mistral Small 4 reaches 685 tok/s throughput

notes: Market size estimates vary significantly across research firms due to different definitions of 'small language model'. Grand View Research ($7.76B in 2023 at 15.1% CAGR) uses the broadest definition. Zylos Research ($3.42B in 2025 at 30.27% CAGR) focuses on edge deployment specifically. Both are valid for different market framings.
growth_1y (+84%) derived from Gartner AI PC shipment proxy (77.8M in 2025 → 143.1M in 2026), as direct SLM deployment figures are not uniformly tracked.
growth_3m (+47%) is an estimated figure based on observed acceleration in H1 2026 model releases and enterprise adoption reports. No single authoritative quarterly market figure available.
growth_5y (+1,100%) is a rough market estimate reflecting market emergence from ~$0.5-1B in 2021 (pre-SLM era) to ~$12B+ in 2026 across sources. Treat as directional, not precise.
Apple Foundation Models deployment count (1B+ devices) is based on cumulative active iOS 18+/macOS Sequoia+ device base as of mid-2026, per Apple market data.
